description:
You are a data-center log analyst who has just obtained three compressed latency logs – one per geographical
  region – and you must determine an appropriate global
  “daily latency cap” for the whole platform.

  1.  INPUT LOCATION  
      • All input files are already present under  
        /home/user/data/traffic_logs/  
        They are named:  
        - region1.log.gz  
        - region2.log.gz  
        - region3.log.gz  

  2.  RAW-LOG FORMAT (inside every *.log.gz*)  
      One entry per line, exactly in this form (no extra spaces):  
        [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] <latency_in_ms>  
      Example:  
        [2023-05-01 08:15:23] 120  

  3.  WHAT YOU MUST DELIVER  
      Create a new directory exactly named /home/user/output/  
      and inside it generate ALL THREE of the following artefacts:

      A)  /home/user/output/latency_summary.csv  
          CSV header must be the first line, literal text:  
          Date,TotalRequests,MeanLatencyMs,95thPercentileLatencyMs  
          One subsequent line per calendar date in chronological order (ascending),
          values comma-separated, **no whitespace other than the commas**.  
          Example layout (values will, of course, differ):  
            Date,TotalRequests,MeanLatencyMs,95thPercentileLatencyMs  
            2023-05-01,42,113,150  
            2023-05-02,37,120,155  

      B)  /home/user/output/latency_cap.json  
          Strict JSON, single object, two numeric keys:  
            {
              "optimal_cap_ms": <integer>,
              "objective_value": <float_rounded_to_2_decimal_places>
            }  
          –  optimal_cap_ms must be an integer between **100** and **160** (inclusive).  
          –  objective_value must be the final value of the objective
             defined below, rounded to exactly two decimal places.  

      C)  /home/user/output/process.log  
          A plain-text chronological record of what you did.
          Each line must start with an RFC-3339 timestamp,
          followed by “ – ” (space, en-dash, space) and a short message.  
          The log must contain at least the three milestones:  
            • “Extraction completed”  
            • “Summary created”  
            • “Optimization done”  

  4.  OPTIMISATION TASK  
      For every calendar date, let μ_d be the MeanLatencyMs from your CSV.  
      You must choose an integer cap C (100 ≤ C ≤ 160) that minimises

           Σ_d  max(0, μ_d − C)²

      (sum of squared exceedances).  
      Any solver is acceptable (SciPy, pulp, cvxpy, custom brute force, etc.),
      but the final answer must be written exactly as described in 3B.

  5.  PARALLELISM REQUIREMENT  
      Decompress the three *.log.gz* files **in parallel** (background jobs or GNU parallel);
      the automated evaluator will later inspect your *process.log* for evidence that
      at least two decompressions overlapped in time.

  6.  VERIFICATION HINTS  
      • Do **not** hard-code any numbers in advance—derive everything from the logs.  
      • The evaluator will:  
        – Check that /home/user/output/ exists.  
        – Verify the exact filenames.  
        – Parse your CSV and recompute means & percentiles.  
        – Recompute the objective with your chosen cap.  
        – Inspect process.log for the required milestone texts and overlapping
          decompression timestamps.  

  7.  PERMISSIONS  
      The /home/user/output/ directory must end up world-readable
      (chmod 755 is fine) and every file inside it world-readable (644).  

  8.  WHEN YOU ARE FINISHED  
      Make sure all three deliverables are in place and
      display their paths (one per line) to the console so the reviewer can spot-check quickly.
truth:
<!--  Ground-truth data for automated tests (NOT visible to the agent)  -->

  <!-- 1.  Input files that are present before the task starts -->
  <initial_files>
    <file path="/home/user/data/traffic_logs/region1.log.gz">
      contents_gzipped><![CDATA[
[2023-05-01 08:15:23] 120
[2023-05-01 09:12:11] 110
[2023-05-02 10:03:42] 140
[2023-05-02 11:33:08] 150
[2023-05-03 08:01:55] 200
]]></contents_gzipped>
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/data/traffic_logs/region2.log.gz">
      contents_gzipped><![CDATA[
[2023-05-01 07:55:03] 100
[2023-05-01 12:34:55] 130
[2023-05-02 13:22:11] 160
[2023-05-03 09:45:31] 190
[2023-05-03 19:25:00] 210
]]></contents_gzipped>
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/data/traffic_logs/region3.log.gz">
      contents_gzipped><![CDATA[
[2023-05-01 06:45:13] 115
[2023-05-02 14:11:09] 155
[2023-05-02 15:22:05] 165
[2023-05-03 10:33:59] 205
[2023-05-03 11:00:22] 195
]]></contents_gzipped>
    </file>
  </initial_files>

  <!-- 2.  Expected artefacts after successful completion -->
  <expected_outputs>
    <file path="/home/user/output/latency_summary.csv">
      <content><![CDATA[
Date,TotalRequests,MeanLatencyMs,95thPercentileLatencyMs
2023-05-01,5,115,130
2023-05-02,5,154,165
2023-05-03,5,200,210
]]></content>
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/output/latency_cap.json">
      <content>{
  "optimal_cap_ms": 160,
  "objective_value": 1600.00
}</content>
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/output/process.log">
      <!--  Only key phrases are tested; timestamps may differ -->
      <must_contain>
        <line_substring>Extraction completed</line_substring>
        <line_substring>Summary created</line_substring>
        <line_substring>Optimization done</line_substring>
      </must_contain>
      <parallel_check>true</parallel_check>
    </file>
  </expected_outputs>

  <!-- 3.  Permissions that will be verified -->
  <permissions>
    <dir path="/home/user/output" mode="755"/>
    <file path="/home/user/output/latency_summary.csv" mode="644"/>
    <file path="/home/user/output/latency_cap.json" mode="644"/>
    <file path="/home/user/output/process.log" mode="644"/>
  </permissions>
